attribution: GTFS Scorecard (gtfsscorecard.org), scored on top of the MobilityData gtfs-validator

problems.effort: Often one default setting in your export.
problems.fix: Set wheelchair_accessible on every trip (most small-agency fleets are 100% accessible, so this is often a single default).
problems.what: 2295 of 2295 trips don't say whether the vehicle is wheelchair accessible.
problems.why: Even with accessible stops, riders need to know the bus itself can take them.

problems.effort: A column in stops.txt; your scheduling software likely has it.
problems.fix: Set wheelchair_boarding to 1 (accessible) or 2 (not accessible) for every stop. A field survey can start with the busiest stops.
problems.what: 566 of 566 stops don't say whether a wheelchair user can board there.
problems.why: Riders who use wheelchairs can't plan a trip when accessibility is marked 'unknown'; apps show no information at all.

problems.effort: Often a bulk fix in your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Use mixed case for stop names and headsigns (e.g. 'Main St & 2nd Ave', not 'MAIN ST & 2ND AVE').
problems.what: Some rider-facing names are in ALL CAPS or all lowercase.
problems.why: ALL-CAPS stop and headsign names are harder to read in apps and are read awkwardly by screen readers.

problems.effort: A small file for most flat-fare systems.
problems.fix: Add fare_attributes.txt (or Fares v2 files) with your fare structure. If your service is fare-free, ask to have it marked fare-free instead.
problems.what: The feed contains no fare information.
problems.why: Riders see 'fare unknown' in trip planners and can't budget their trip; visitors are most affected.

problems.effort: A few minutes per flagged trip.
problems.fix: Check the flagged stop times for transposed minutes.
problems.what: Some scheduled trips travel implausibly fast between stops.
problems.why: Usually a typo'd stop time; riders get impossible arrival estimates.

problems.fix: Enable 'remove unused shapes' (or similar) in your export tool.
problems.what: The feed contains route shapes no trip uses.
problems.why: Harmless to riders, but it bloats the feed and suggests stale export data.

problems.effort: A quick look at the flagged files.
problems.fix: Check the flagged column names for misspellings of standard GTFS fields; remove them if they are vendor extras.
problems.what: Some files contain columns that are not part of the GTFS spec.
problems.why: Harmless to riders, but apps ignore these columns and they can hide typos in real column names.

problems.effort: A review pass in your scheduling software.
problems.fix: Remove retired stops from the export, or add them back to the trips that should serve them.
problems.what: Some stops exist in the feed but no trip ever stops at them.
problems.why: Riders may walk to a stop where no bus is scheduled to arrive.

problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'same_name_and_description_for_route' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Same name and description for route (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.fix: Check with your AVL vendor that every vehicle assignment flows into TripUpdates, including school-day and tripper runs.
problems.what: 2 of 9 trips scheduled during the sampling window had no live predictions.
problems.why: Riders on those trips get schedule data dressed up as realtime. Caltrans expects every operating trip in TripUpdates.

problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'equal_shape_distance_same_coordinates' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Equal shape distance same coordinates (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.

problems.effort: One small file, set once in export settings.
problems.fix: Add feed_info.txt with publisher name, URL, language, and feed_start_date/feed_end_date.
problems.what: A recommended GTFS file (usually feed_info.txt) is missing.
problems.why: feed_info.txt tells apps who publishes the feed and when it expires; without it nobody is warned before data goes stale.

problems.effort: Two fields, set once in export settings.
problems.fix: Add feed_info.txt with feed_start_date and feed_end_date to your export.
problems.what: feed_info.txt is missing its start/end dates (the file itself is absent)
problems.why: Apps and this scorecard can't warn anyone before the feed goes stale without stated validity dates.

problems.what: feed_info.txt has no technical contact (feed_contact_email or feed_contact_url).
problems.why: App makers and state data programs have nobody to email when they spot a problem with your feed, so problems linger.

problems.effort: Worth it for multi-level or large stations; flat stops don't need it.
problems.fix: Add pathways.txt connecting entrances, platforms, and any elevators, with a level for each.
problems.what: This feed models stations or entrances but has no pathways.txt.
problems.why: Trip planners can't guide riders through the station, and there is no step-free route information for wheelchair users.

problems.fix: Review the rule documentation for 'service_window_outside_feed_period' at https://gtfs-validator.mobilitydata.org/rules.html and check the flagged rows in your feed.
problems.what: Service window outside feed period (flagged by the MobilityData validator).
problems.why: See the linked rule for what this affects.
